name: Nippon India ETF Nifty PSU Bank

index.name: Nifty PSU Bank Index
index.description: Nifty PSU Bank tracks 12 government-owned banks controlling 45% of banking system deposits. Post-2014 transformation: NPA cleanup (peak NPAs ₹12 Lakh Cr in 2019 now ₹8 Lakh Cr), ₹3.5 Lakh Cr government recapitalization, SARFAESI debt recovery measures accelerated. ROE recovered from sub-5% (2016-2019) to 12-15% (2023-2024), approaching private banks' 15-18%. Turnaround potential: when ROE normalizes to 15%+ (2-3 years runway), PSU bank valuations will re-rate from 1.2x book value to 1.5-1.8x book value (40-50% upside). Dividend yield 5-6% provides income cushion. State Bank of India dominance: 34.6% weight, ₹55 Lakh Cr assets, ₹23K branches. YONO super-app digitalization reducing branch dependency while expanding reach. Structural advantage: Priority sector lending mandates (40% of advances to agriculture, MSME, housing) ensure government support. Currency tailwind: deposits concentrated in rupee, loans in rupee (net rupee neutral), but government guarantee on deposits attracts foreign investors during currency crises. Capex efficiency: each rupee invested generates ₹8-10 annual interest income vs private banks' ₹15-20 due to lower risk profile and lower ROE targets.
